mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 5.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and stable consumer prices indicates robust real purchasing power growth, which directly fuels corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and stable CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity repricing and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery regimes yield negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding construction activity and rising household wealth effects, which systematically boost consumer confidence and corporate capital expenditure, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in >65% of cases, or the correlation between HOUST growth and forward equity returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the threshold effect disappears in a logistic regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, a quarterly decline in real GDP signals an imminent economic contraction, prompting institutional investors to de-risk equity portfolios and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.
